name: Willie "The Lion" Smith
abstract: William Henry Joseph Bonaparte Bertholf Smith (November 23, 1893 – April 18, 1973), nicknamed "the Lion", was an American jazz and stride pianist.

concerts.label: Carnegie Pop Concert: Salute to Fats Waller
Midnight Performance: Dody Goodman in "Dody in Dixieland"
Jazz Concert in Memory of Sidney Bechet
Midnight Performance: Salute to Eddie Condon
Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Art Tatum: So-Lo Piano

works.t: Here Comes the Band (Bring On the Band)

about_quotes.speaker: Rossano Sportiello
about_quotes.evidence: Even Willie “The Lion” Smith, the great stride piano player, he said that the greatest tune ever written was the “St. Louis Blues.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Rossano Sportiello interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 2, 2015 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: He took me back to Willie “The Lion” Smith and James P. Johnson and Louis Armstrong. I used to have to transcribe their solos.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: I interviewed Willie about 1970... So I told him I wanted to interview him about Jelly... So I said I'd be glad to pay him $50 for the interview... He was very nice. He talked for an hour.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Saturday night, the 26th of April I played this session with Dicky Wells, Eddie Barefield, the great Willie "the Lion" Smith and two drummers, Jo Jones and Panama Francis.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: one of the jobs that I had in the earliest, was with Willie 'the Lion' Smith when he added me to his duo to make a trio.

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Rollins
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t know, I would imagine that Bud knew about Willie. I don’t know if Willie knew about Bud. I mean, he may have, but I know Bud–‘cause everybody knew Willie “The Lion” Smith, see–so, Bud knew that Willie “The Lion” lived up the street on St. Nicholas Avenue, yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sonny Rollins — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
